components.name: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate trihydrate (K2HPO4·3H2O)
components.evidence: N−C− minimal medium (Csonka et al, 1994), containing K2SO4 (1 g), K2HPO4·3H2O (17.7 g), KH2PO4 (4.7 g), MgSO4·7H2O (0.1 g), and NaCl (2.5 g) per liter.

components.name: potassium dihydrogen phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: containing K2SO4 (1 g), K2HPO4·3H2O (17.7 g), KH2PO4 (4.7 g), MgSO4·7H2O (0.1 g), and NaCl (2.5 g) per liter.

components.name: potassium sulfate (K2SO4)
components.evidence: containing K2SO4 (1 g), K2HPO4·3H2O (17.7 g), KH2PO4 (4.7 g), MgSO4·7H2O (0.1 g), and NaCl (2.5 g) per liter.

components.name: magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO4·7H2O)
components.evidence: containing K2SO4 (1 g), K2HPO4·3H2O (17.7 g), KH2PO4 (4.7 g), MgSO4·7H2O (0.1 g), and NaCl (2.5 g) per liter.

components.name: sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.evidence: containing K2SO4 (1 g), K2HPO4·3H2O (17.7 g), KH2PO4 (4.7 g), MgSO4·7H2O (0.1 g), and NaCl (2.5 g) per liter.

components.name: ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.evidence: The medium was supplemented with 20 mM NH4Cl, as nitrogen source, and 5 mM glycerol, as the sole carbon source.

components.name: glycerol
components.evidence: The medium was supplemented with 20 mM NH4Cl, as nitrogen source, and 5 mM glycerol, as the sole carbon source.

components.name: oxygen (O2)
components.evidence: For growth in batch cultures, the same protocol described in Schink et al, 2019 was applied. However, due to acetate excretion in the medium in the pre[culture]... (aerobic shaking batch cultures of E. coli).

media.base_medium: N−C− minimal medium (Csonka LN, Ikeda TP, Fletcher SA, Kustu S, 1994, J Bacteriol 176:6324–6333)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as Csonka et al 1994 (N−C− minimal medium, J Bacteriol 176:6324–6333, PMC196974)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch PMC196974 (full text only as scanned page images, not OCR'd); cross-confirmed the identical salt recipe in two independent same-lab papers that reproduce N−C− verbatim (WebFetch PMC10477958 and this paper's Culture-medium section)

sources_chased.what: growth protocol reference Schink et al 2019 (Cell Systems 9:64–73) for batch-culture conditions / oxygenation
sources_chased.how: WebSearch confirmed same aerobic batch-culture N−C− protocol

media.notes: The medium is fully defined in the paper's 'Culture medium' section and is already complete at the ionic level: the current exchange list (Cl, glycerol, K, Mg, Na, NH4, O2, Pi, SO4) captures every species contributed by the five salts plus the N and C sources. N−C− minimal medium (Csonka/Kustu) is a phosphate-buffered defined salts medium that intentionally contains NO added trace-metal/micronutrient mix, NO CaCl2, and NO vitamins/thiamine — verified by convergent reproduction of the identical recipe in independent papers; growth relies on trace-metal contaminants. 1 mM IPTG was added 'when necessary' to induce the lac operon; it is an inducer, not a nutrient, so it is not listed as a medium component. All chemicals were from Carl Roth. Reported concentrations converted from g/L using MW: K2HPO4·3H2O 228.22, KH2PO4 136.09, K2SO4 174.26, MgSO4·7H2O 246.47, NaCl 58.44.
